mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150k signals robust labor market momentum and consumer income expansion, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:October 2017 payroll growth remained solid at 132.8M persons employed
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP growth below 2.0% signals weakening economic momentum and reduced corporate profit expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as equity valuations compress.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Q2 2017 real GDP at 13429.0B chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 QoQ growth is below 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.5% while core inflation (proxied by CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.5%, the Fed is in a restrictive tightening cycle that raises discount rates and increases refinancing risk for equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:September 2017 FEDFUNDS at 0.13% and CPIAUCSL at 229.098
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with contained inflation
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC is not significantly higher than either single-series IC (difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1.0M annualized units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, which reduces construction employment and consumer wealth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1.0M units annualized for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 2.0% year-over-year while unemployment remains stable below 5.0%, signaling supply-side capacity expansion without wage-driven inflation, which supports profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2017 INDPRO at 96.2095 and September 2017 UNRATE at 8.2%
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC is not significantly higher than either single-series IC (difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month by more than 1.0% while unemployment rises simultaneously, signaling a demand shock and consumer retrenchment that reduces forward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth < -1.0% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
regime_hint: demand shock with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal's Rank IC is not significantly higher than RSAFS alone (difference < 0.015).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 6-month rise in the federal funds rate above 1.0% combined with declining real retail sales signals monetary tightening that is beginning to constrain demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 1.0% over 6 months AND RSAFS YoY growth falls below 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:September 2017 FEDFUNDS at 0.13% and August 2017 RSAFS at 407.8B
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with demand deceleration
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in less than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC is not significantly higher than either single-series IC (difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration and forward-looking recession risk, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration signaling recession risk
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
